docs.title: Platforms
docs.text: reactor-uc supports multiple embedded platforms through dedicated template repositories. Each template provides a pre-configured project structure with build system integration, example applications, and platform-specific setup.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
To start a new project:

  1. Create a repository from the template on GitHub
  2. Clone with submodules: git clone --recurse-submodules <your-repo>
  3. Set REACTOR_UC_PATH to your reactor-uc installation
  4. Follow the platform-specific build instructions

docs.title: Template Repositories
docs.text: Platform Template Build System Zephyr lf-zephyr-uc-template West RIOT lf-riot-uc-template Make Pico lf-pico-uc-template CMake FreeRTOS lf-freertos-uc-template CMake ESP-IDF lf-esp-idf-uc-template CMake + ESP-IDF Patmos lf-patmos-template Make

docs.title: Common Prerequisites
docs.text:
All platforms require:

  • Linux or macOS (some support Windows via WSL)
  • Git for version control
  • Java 17 for the Lingua Franca compiler
  • reactor-uc cloned with REACTOR_UC_PATH environment variable set
git clone https://github.com/lf-lang/reactor-uc.git --recurse-submodules
export REACTOR_UC_PATH=/path/to/reactor-uc

docs.title: ESP-IDF
docs.text:
• Template: lf-esp-idf-uc-template
  • ESP-IDF Documentation: docs.espressif.com
  • Supported Boards: ESP32, ESP32-C3, ESP32-C6, ESP32-S2, ESP32-S3, ESP32-H2

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
In addition to the common prerequisites:

  • Python 3.8+
  • CMake 3.20+
  • Ninja build system

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Clone and initialize submodules:

git clone https://github.com/lf-lang/lf-esp-idf-uc-template.git my-project
cd my-project
git submodule update --init --recursive

docs.title: Option A: Using Nix (Recommended)
docs.text:
nix develop           # Default: ESP32
nix develop .#esp32c3 # ESP32-C3
nix develop .#esp32c6 # ESP32-C6

docs.title: Option B: Manual Setup
docs.text:
cd esp-idf
./install.sh esp32c6  # Or: esp32, esp32c3, all, etc.
source ./export.sh
cd ..

Source export.sh in each new terminal:

source ./esp-idf/export.sh

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
Set the target board and configure:

export ESP_BOARD=esp32c6

cmake -Bbuild \
      -DESP_IDF_BOARD=$ESP_BOARD \
      -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=$IDF_PATH/tools/cmake/toolchain-$ESP_BOARD.cmake \
      -GNinja

Build:

cmake --build build -j $(nproc)

docs.title: Flashing
docs.text:
cd build
ninja flash

Or specify the port:

ninja flash -p /dev/ttyUSB0

Flash and monitor:

ninja flash && ninja monitor

Press Ctrl+] to exit the monitor.

docs.title: Changing the Application
docs.text:
cmake -Bbuild \
      -DLF_MAIN=Timer \
      -DLOG_LEVEL=LF_LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG \
      -DESP_IDF_BOARD=$ESP_BOARD \
      -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=$IDF_PATH/tools/cmake/toolchain-$ESP_BOARD.cmake \
      -GNinja

docs.title: Build Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description LF_MAIN LF file to compile (default: HelloEsp) LOG_LEVEL LF_LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG ESP_IDF_BOARD esp32, esp32c3, esp32c6, esp32s2, esp32s3, esp32h2

docs.title: Cleaning
docs.text: rm -rf src-gen build

docs.title: Example Applications
docs.text:
• HelloEsp - Serial console output
  • Timer - Periodic timer events
  • Blink - LED toggle

docs.title: FreeRTOS
docs.text:
• Template: lf-freertos-uc-template
  • FreeRTOS Documentation: freertos.org
  • Supported Devices: FreeRTOS Supported Devices

docs.title: Supported Platforms
docs.text:
The FreeRTOS template currently supports:

  • Pico (RP2040/RP2350) - Raspberry Pi Pico and Pico 2

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Clone and initialize submodules:

git clone https://github.com/lf-lang/lf-freertos-uc-template.git my-project
cd my-project
git submodule update --init --recursive

Install the ARM toolchain as described in the Pico platform prerequisites.

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
mkdir build && cd build
cmake -DPLATFORM_TARGET=pico ..
make

docs.title: Flashing (Pico)
docs.text:
Put the Pico into BOOTSEL mode, then:

picotool load -x bin/Blink.elf

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
lf-freertos-uc-template/
├── CMakeLists.txt
├── platforms/
│   └── pico/
│       ├── FreeRTOSConfig.h
│       ├── platform.cmake
│       └── README.md
├── src/
│   ├── Blink.lf
│   ├── HelloFreeRTOS.lf
│   └── Timer.lf
└── FreeRTOS/                # Submodule

docs.title: Platform Configuration
docs.text:
Each platform has its own FreeRTOSConfig.h for tuning:

  • CPU clock: 133 MHz (Pico default)
  • Tick rate: 100 Hz
  • Heap size: 128 KB

docs.title: Adding New Platforms
docs.text:
1. Create platforms/<platform-name>/
  2. Add platform.cmake with platform-specific CMake configuration
  3. Add FreeRTOSConfig.h tuned for your hardware
  4. Optionally add FreeRTOS hooks in <platform>_freertos_hooks.c

docs.title: Pico-Specific Features
docs.text:
• USB and UART stdio enabled by default
  • Pico W WiFi libraries automatically linked
  • Connect GPIO 0 (TX) and GPIO 1 (RX) for UART output

docs.title: Patmos
docs.text:
• Template: lf-patmos-template
  • Patmos Project: patmos-project

Patmos is a time-predictable processor designed for hard real-time systems and WCET (Worst-Case Execution Time) analysis. reactor-uc supports single-threaded static scheduling on Patmos.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Patmos toolchain (patmos-clang compiler)
  • Pasim simulator
  • Platin WCET analysis tool

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Clone the required repositories:

# Clone the Patmos template
git clone https://github.com/lf-lang/lf-patmos-template.git

# Clone Lingua Franca and checkout the static schedule branch
git clone https://github.com/lf-lang/lingua-franca.git
cd lingua-franca
git checkout static-schedule-single-thread
git submodule update --init core/src/main/resources/lib/c/reactor-c
cd ..

Copy support files:

cp -r lf-patmos-template/patmos lingua-franca/test/C/src/static/patmos
cp lf-patmos-template/Makefile lingua-franca/test/C/Makefile
cp lf-patmos-template/ADASModel.lf lingua-franca/test/C/src/static/ADASModel.lf
cp lf-patmos-template/SimpleConnection.lf lingua-franca/test/C/src/static/SimpleConnection.lf

docs.title: Building and Running
docs.text:
Navigate to the test directory:

cd lingua-franca/test/C

Build and simulate:

make APP=SingleConnection

This will:

  1. Generate C code using lfc
  2. Compile with the Patmos compiler
  3. Simulate with Pasim
  4. Analyze with Platin

docs.title: WCET Analysis
docs.text:
Analyze a specific function:

make APP=SingleConnection FUNC=_sinkreaction_function_0 wcet

docs.title: Make Targets
docs.text: Target Description gen Generate C code using lfc copy Copy support files to src-gen comp Compile with Patmos compiler sim Simulate with Pasim wcet Analyze WCET with Platin clean Delete compiled files del Delete all generated code

docs.title: Static Scheduling
docs.text: Patmos uses reactor-uc's static scheduler for predictable execution timing. The schedule is computed at compile time, enabling precise WCET analysis.

docs.title: Raspberry Pi Pico
docs.text:
• Template: lf-pico-uc-template
  • Pico SDK: github.com/raspberrypi/pico-sdk
  • Documentation: raspberrypi.com/documentation/pico-sdk
  • Supported Boards: Pico Series

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Install the ARM toolchain: Install ARM Cross-Compiler

Install picotool: Install Picotool

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Clone and initialize submodules:

git clone https://github.com/lf-lang/lf-pico-uc-template.git my-project
cd my-project
git submodule update --init --recursive

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
Configure and build:

cmake -Bbuild
cmake --build build -j $(nproc)

This creates build/Blink.elf.

docs.title: Flashing
docs.text:
Put the Pico into BOOTSEL mode (hold BOOTSEL while connecting USB), then:

picotool load -x build/Blink.elf

docs.title: Changing the Application
docs.text:
Edit LF_MAIN in CMakeLists.txt or via command line:

cmake -Bbuild -DLF_MAIN=Timer
cmake --build build -j $(nproc)

docs.title: Log Level
docs.text: cmake -Bbuild -DLOG_LEVEL=LF_LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG

docs.title: Board Variants
docs.text:
For Pico 2 (RP2350):

export PICO_BOARD=pico2
cmake -Bbuild

For Pico W:

export PICO_BOARD=pico_w
cmake -Bbuild

docs.title: Cleaning
docs.text: rm -rf src-gen build

docs.text: Channel Mode Description UART Polled Serial point-to-point

docs.title: RIOT
docs.text:
• Template: lf-riot-uc-template
  • RIOT Documentation: doc.riot-os.org
  • Supported Boards: RIOT Board Support

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
In addition to the common prerequisites:

  • make version 4.0+ (on macOS, install via brew install make and use gmake)
  • ARM cross-compiler for ARM-based boards:
# Debian/Ubuntu
sudo apt install gcc-arm-none-eabi

# Or use Nix
nix develop

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Clone and initialize submodules:

git clone https://github.com/lf-lang/lf-riot-uc-template.git my-project
cd my-project
git submodule update --init --recursive

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
Configure LF_MAIN and BOARD in the Makefile, then:

make all

Or override on command line:

make LF_MAIN=HelloWorld BOARD=nucleo-f446re all

For native Linux simulation:

make BOARD=native all

docs.title: Flashing
docs.text:
make flash

Or with parameters:

make LF_MAIN=HelloWorld BOARD=nucleo-f446re flash

docs.title: Terminal
docs.text:
Open a serial terminal:

make term

docs.title: Log Level
docs.text:
Edit the Makefile:

CFLAGS += -DLF_LOG_LEVEL_ALL=LF_LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG

docs.title: Network Channels
docs.text: Channel Mode Description TCP/IP Async TCP sockets via RIOT's GNRC stack CoAP/UDP Async CoAP over UDP for IoT networks UART Polled, Async Serial point-to-point

docs.title: macOS Notes
docs.text:
Use gmake instead of make (macOS ships with make 3.81, RIOT requires 4.0+):

brew install make
gmake all

docs.title: Zephyr
docs.text:
• Template: lf-zephyr-uc-template
  • Zephyr Documentation: docs.zephyrproject.org
  • Supported Boards: Zephyr Board Index

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Install Zephyr dependencies: Install Dependencies

  2. Install the Zephyr SDK: Install the Zephyr SDK

  3. Create and activate a Python virtual environment:

python3 -m venv ./venv
source ./venv/bin/activate
  1. Install west and fetch Zephyr sources:
pip install west
west update
pip install -r deps/zephyr/scripts/requirements.txt
west zephyr-export

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
Build and run using the native_posix target (emulation):

west build -t run

Build for a specific board:

west build -b qemu_cortex_m3 -p always -t run
west build -b adafruit_feather -p always

The -p always flag cleans the build directory when changing target boards.

docs.title: Changing the Application
docs.text:
Set the LF_MAIN CMake variable in CMakeLists.txt or via command line:

west build -b adafruit_feather -p always -- -DLF_MAIN=Blinky

docs.title: Log Level
docs.text: west build -t run -p always -- -DLOG_LEVEL=LF_LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG

docs.title: Flashing
docs.text: west flash

docs.title: Cleaning
docs.text: west clean

docs.title: Network Channels
docs.text: Channel Description TCP/IP TCP sockets over Ethernet/WiFi

docs.title: NXP FRDM K64F Distributed Computing
docs.text:
federated reactor {
    @platform_zephyr
    node_0 = new FRDMK64FBoard();
    @platform_zephyr
    node_1 = new FRDMK64FBoard();
    @platform_zephyr

}

docs.title: Redundant Picos
docs.text: A fault-tolerant flight controller using federated reactor-uc execution across multiple Raspberry Pi Pico boards.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This project demonstrates hardware redundancy using reactor-uc's federated execution capabilities. Multiple Pico boards run as federated nodes, providing failover capability for safety-critical flight control applications.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
The system uses a redundant node architecture with automatic failover:

┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│   Node 0    │     │   Node 1    │     │   Node 2    │
│  (Primary)  │◄───►│  (Backup)   │◄───►│  (Backup)   │
│             │     │             │     │             │
│ FlightCtrl  │     │ FlightCtrl  │     │ FlightCtrl  │
│  Sensors    │     │  Sensors    │     │  Sensors    │
└─────────────┘     └─────────────┘     └─────────────┘
       │                  │                   │
       └──────────────────┴───────────────────┘
                          │
                    ┌─────▼─────┐
                    │  Actuators │
                    └───────────┘

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text: Reactor Purpose FlightControllerReal Main flight control logic RedundantNode Redundancy and node coordination Failover Automatic failover detection and switching Forwarder Inter-node message routing SensorTest Sensor validation and testing

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
Clone the repository and build for a specific federated node:

git clone https://github.com/tanneberger/fault-tolerance-testing.git
cd fault-tolerance-testing

Build and flash each node:

# Node 0 (Primary)
cmake -Bbuild -DLF_MAIN=FlightControllerReal -DFEDERATE=node_0
cmake --build build
sudo picotool load -x build/LF_MAIN.elf

# Node 1 (Backup)
cmake -Bbuild -DLF_MAIN=FlightControllerReal -DFEDERATE=node_1
cmake --build build
sudo picotool load -x build/LF_MAIN.elf

For sensor testing:

cmake -Bbuild -DLF_MAIN=SensorTest
cmake --build build
sudo picotool load -x build/LF_MAIN.elf
